core_payload.content:
# CapsuleOS Law Surfaces

## 1. Role
CapsuleOS Law Surfaces owns the explicit constitutional artifacts that define what a legal capsule is. It keeps the five-element contract, gate law, semantic-hash discipline, schema shape, and specification text together so the operating system stays inspectable as law instead of folklore.

## 2. Law Surface
This sub-hub groups `capsule-generation-protocol`, `capsuleos.semantic-hash`, `capsuleos-schema`, `capsuleos-spec`, `capsuleos.5-element-law`, and `capsuleos.16-gates`. Together they define capsule shape, canonical hashing posture, validator gate law, and the repo-readable specification that humans and agents both rely on.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own branch posture decisions, human or agent maintenance interfaces, or storage and audit substrate. Those responsibilities belong to `admission-lifecycle`, `stewardship-interfaces`, and `runtime-substrate`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: CapsuleOS Law Surfaces groups the constitutional artifacts of the operating system: Capsule Generation Protocol, semantic-hash law, schema, spec, five-element law, and sixteen-gate law. It keeps capsule legality, shape, hashing discipline, and written specification together so the vault can read its trust boundary as explicit law rather than folklore. This boundary matters because every later validator, branch, and maintenance decision depends on these constitutional surfaces staying coherent and easy to inspect.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Dream CapsuleOS decomposition to isolate the constitutional law surfaces that define capsule legality, shape, and gate discipline.
